blurb: Delivers public safety, emergency management, and victim services. Oversees policing standards, emergency preparedness and response, disaster recovery support, victim assistance, and provincial correctional facilities to keep Albertans safe and supported.

goals.title: OUTCOME 1: Albertans are safe in their communities
goals.description: The ministry oversees policing standards, community safety programs, crime prevention initiatives, and public complaint processes. Works with police services to maintain public safety and responds to community concerns about policing.

foundational_services.name: Oversee policing standards and community safety programs
foundational_services.desc: Sets and enforces policing standards across Alberta, coordinates community safety initiatives, manages police conduct oversight, and supports crime prevention programs to maintain public confidence in law enforcement.
foundational_services.users: Police services, community safety coordinators, oversight bodies

relationships.service: File a complaint about police conduct

main_services.name: Report a safety concern or get crime prevention information

supporting.name: Report a property or neighbourhood safety concern through SCAN
supporting.desc: Residents can report property crimes, neighbourhood safety concerns, or suspicious activity to police through the SCAN (Suspicious Activity) program. Reports help police identify crime patterns and deploy resources to address community concerns.

reach_estimate.estimate: Thousands of reports annually from neighbourhoods across Alberta
reach_estimate.basis: Community policing programs receive reports from all major urban and rural areas

economic_impact.note: Effective crime reporting and prevention directly reduces property losses and criminal activity

supporting.name: Access crime prevention resources and community safety programs
supporting.desc: Albertans can access information and resources about crime prevention, personal safety, home security, and community safety programs. Includes guidance on cybercrime prevention, fraud awareness, and neighbourhood safety initiatives.

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of thousands of Albertans access crime prevention information annually
reach_estimate.basis: Province-wide availability and public interest in personal and property safety

economic_impact.note: Crime prevention education reduces victimization and criminal activity, lowering costs to society

supporting.name: Report poaching, illegal hunting, or a wildlife concern
supporting.desc: Albertans can report wildlife crimes including poaching, illegal hunting, or illegal trapping through the dedicated Report a Poacher line. Wildlife officers investigate reports to protect Alberta's wildlife resources and enforce hunting and trapping regulations.

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of wildlife crime reports annually
reach_estimate.basis: Active enforcement line receives reports across the province

economic_impact.note: Wildlife protection enforcement maintains ecological balance and supports hunting heritage

main_services.name: Access policing oversight and complaint processes

supporting.name: File a complaint about police conduct
supporting.desc: Albertans who believe a police officer has engaged in misconduct can file a formal complaint through the independent police complaint process. Complaints are investigated and decided by an impartial body to ensure police accountability.
supporting.users: Members of the public with concerns about police conduct

relationships.service: Oversee policing standards and community safety programs

reach_estimate.basis: Complaints mechanisms are available to all Albertans with concerns about police conduct

economic_impact.note: Independent complaints process maintains public trust in policing and ensures officer accountability

supporting.name: Appeal a police complaint decision
supporting.desc: Complainants can appeal a police complaint decision if they disagree with the outcome of their initial complaint. The appeal process provides a further level of independent review and oversight.
supporting.users: Individuals who have filed police complaints

relationships.service: Oversee policing standards and community safety programs

reach_estimate.estimate: Dozens to low hundreds of appeals annually
reach_estimate.basis: Appeal process available to those dissatisfied with initial complaint decisions

economic_impact.note: Appeal mechanisms strengthen oversight credibility and ensure fairness in complaint resolution

goals.title: OUTCOME 2: Albertans are prepared for and recover from emergencies and disasters
goals.description: The ministry coordinates emergency preparedness, response, and recovery across Alberta. Works with communities and partner agencies to prepare for hazards, respond to emergencies and disasters, and support recovery from major events.

foundational_services.name: Coordinate provincial emergency management and disaster response
foundational_services.desc: Leads provincial emergency preparedness, response, and recovery coordination. Develops emergency plans, manages disaster response operations, allocates recovery resources, and works with municipalities and federal partners during emergencies.
foundational_services.users: Municipal emergency coordinators, emergency responders, disaster recovery planners

main_services.name: Prepare for emergencies and access alerts

supporting.name: Get emergency preparedness information and make a plan
supporting.desc: Albertans can access emergency preparedness information, including how to prepare for natural disasters, severe weather, and other emergencies. Resources include family emergency planning guides, emergency supply checklists, and guidance for vulnerable populations.
supporting.users: Residents, families, seniors, people with disabilities

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of thousands of Albertans access preparedness information
reach_estimate.basis: Province-wide emergency risks and public responsibility for family preparedness

economic_impact.note: Prepared families and communities reduce disaster impact, losses, and recovery time

supporting.name: Sign up for or check Alberta Emergency Alerts
supporting.desc: Albertans can receive emergency alerts about severe weather, wildfires, floods, and other emergencies through Alberta Emergency Alerts. Alerts are sent to registered phones and can be checked online to stay informed of threats and emergency information.
supporting.users: All Albertans, residents in areas affected by emergencies

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of thousands of Albertans registered for alerts
reach_estimate.basis: Statewide system with active uptake during severe weather and emergency seasons

economic_impact.note: Timely emergency alerts enable rapid evacuation, protective action, and life-safety responses

supporting.name: Apply for disaster recovery assistance
supporting.desc: Albertans affected by disasters (floods, wildfires, severe storms) can apply for provincial disaster recovery assistance to help with recovery costs. Applications are accepted through the disaster recovery program and assessed for eligibility and support.

reach_estimate.estimate: Thousands of recovery assistance applications during major disaster events
reach_estimate.basis: Provincial disaster recovery programs available after declared disasters

economic_impact.note: Recovery assistance accelerates economic and community recovery, reducing long-term disaster impacts

supporting.name: Access wildfire or flood status information
supporting.desc: Albertans can check current wildfire and flood status, active incidents, evacuation orders, and impacts to communities and infrastructure. Status information is updated regularly during active incidents to keep the public informed.
supporting.users: Residents in or near affected areas, emergency planners, media

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of thousands of accesses during wildfire and flood seasons
reach_estimate.basis: Critical information for residents in affected areas and provincial emergency coordination

economic_impact.note: Real-time incident information enables rapid evacuation decisions and emergency response coordination

goals.title: OUTCOME 3: Victims and communities are supported through the justice and corrections systems
goals.description: The ministry provides victim services and support throughout the justice and corrections systems. Operates provincial correctional facilities, offers victim assistance programs, facilitates community reintegration, and ensures public safety through corrections.

foundational_services.name: Operate correctional facilities and support community reintegration
foundational_services.desc: Operates provincial correctional facilities, manages inmate custody, coordinates community supervision and reintegration programs, and administers restorative justice initiatives to reduce reoffending and support community safety.
foundational_services.users: Inmates, community supervision clients, victim advocates, reintegration planners

supporting.name: Find victim services in your community
supporting.desc: Crime victims can access a range of support services including victim counselling, crisis support, legal information, and referrals. Services are available across the province through victim services programs and community partners.
supporting.users: Crime victims, family members of victims, survivors

reach_estimate.estimate: Thousands of victims access services annually
reach_estimate.basis: Crime victims across Alberta access counselling, legal support, and recovery services

economic_impact.note: Victim support services reduce trauma impact and facilitate recovery and community reintegration

supporting.name: Access information about an offender in custody or on supervision
supporting.desc: Members of the public and victims can access information about offenders in provincial custody or under community supervision, including release status and conditions. Information is available to support victim safety planning and public awareness.
supporting.users: Crime victims, interested members of the public, community members

reach_estimate.basis: Victims and concerned members of the public access offender release and status information

economic_impact.note: Offender information access supports victim safety planning and community confidence in justice system

supporting.name: Visit an inmate in a provincial correctional facility
supporting.desc: Family members and visitors can arrange visits with inmates held in provincial correctional facilities. Visitor information includes facility locations, visiting schedules, identification requirements, and facility rules.
supporting.users: Family members, friends, legal representatives of inmates

reach_estimate.estimate: Thousands of visits to provincial facilities annually
reach_estimate.basis: Multiple correctional facilities across the province with regular visiting programs

economic_impact.note: Family contact during incarceration supports inmate mental health and improves reintegration outcomes

supporting.name: Access reintegration and community supervision information
supporting.desc: Individuals on community supervision or release can access information about their conditions, supervision requirements, reintegration programs, and community resources. Information supports compliance and successful reintegration into the community.
supporting.users: Individuals on release or community supervision, support workers, community partners

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of individuals access reintegration information monthly
reach_estimate.basis: Community supervision populations and reintegration program participants province-wide

economic_impact.note: Reintegration support reduces recidivism and supports successful community reentry, improving public safety
